pi_items.verified_value: mroz.gdt - Real dataset from T.A. Mroz (1987), 1976 PSID Panel Study of Income Dynamics, based on 1975 data, 753 observations of married women
pi_items.source: https://www.learneconometrics.com/gretl/poe5/data/mroz.gdt (downloadable dataset), http://www.principlesofeconometrics.com/poe5/data/def/mroz.def (official definition), R wooldridge package documentation

pi_items.verified_value: lfp - Binary dummy variable: 1 if woman worked in 1975, else 0. Total of 428 women with positive hours (lfp=1) and 325 women without work (lfp=0) in 753 observations
pi_items.source: gretl mroz.gdt file XML metadata, Principles of Econometrics dataset definition

pi_items.verified_value: Probit - Binary choice model using cumulative normal distribution for female labor force participation prediction
pi_items.source: Task description and econometric standard practice for binary outcomes

pi_items.verified_value: 73.44% - Based on confusion matrix from Wooldridge's Introductory Econometrics textbook example: 348 true positives + 205 true negatives = 553 correct / 753 total observations
pi_items.source: Calculated from probit classification table (TP=348, FP=120, FN=80, TN=205) referenced in Wooldridge textbook econometric examples on Mroz dataset. Web search confirmed these values in Wooldridge's standard teaching examples.

pi_items.metadata_value: const, k5, k618, age, wc, hc, inc
pi_items.verified_value: Standard econometric shorthand variable names that map to gretl dataset variables: const (constant), k5→kidsl6 (children <6), k618→kids618 (children 6-18), age (wife's age), wc→educ (wife's education years), hc→heduc (husband's education years), inc→faminc (family income)
pi_items.source: gretl mroz.gdt file variable definitions, confirmed by agent task execution trajectory artifacts showing explicit variable mapping decisions (kidsl6=k5, kids618=k618, educ=wc, heduc=hc, faminc=inc)

pi_items.verified_value: https://www.learneconometrics.com/gretl/poe5/data/mroz.gdt - URL is live and returns valid gzip-compressed gretl dataset file (HTTP 200, 32067 bytes, last modified 2018-08-29)
pi_items.source: Live URL verification via HTTP HEAD request and successful file download
gretl mroz.gdt XML: endobs='753', Principles of Econometrics website, multiple econometric textbook references

pi_items.source: gretl mroz.gdt description metadata, Principles of Econometrics dataset documentation

privileged_info_summary: The Mroz dataset (mroz.gdt) is a real, non-synthetic dataset containing 753 observations of married women from the 1976 PSID, based on 1975 data (T.A. Mroz, 1987). The binary dependent variable is lfp (labor force participation: 1 if worked in 1975, else 0), with 428 women working and 325 not working. Independent variables use standard econometric shorthand (k5=children<6, k618=children 6-18, age, wc=wife's education years, hc=husband's education years, inc=family income). The Probit model achieves approximately 73.44% classification accuracy based on Wooldridge's textbook example, which falls within the task's expected range of 60.0-75.0%. Output paths are configured in the gretl environment at /home/ga/Documents/gretl_output/.
